title: Epoch reboot coin-flip: random per-boot epoch breaks peers' replay windows ~50% of the time
description:
link_ctx.c draws a random epoch in [128,255] at every boot ('for reboot resilience'). But a peer that REMEMBERS this node's previous epoch stream in its replay window (last_counter = old_epoch<<16|seq) rejects ALL frames from the new boot whenever the new draw is LOWER than the old one - a ~50% coin flip per reboot, per peer. Observed live on the bench 2026-07-17 (per-node keys, no shared-key confound): gateway reflash -> T-Echo rejected every gateway frame as replay (-120, 21 in 185s); resetting the T-Echo fixed its RX window but rerolled its own TX epoch and lost the flip at the GATEWAY's window -> 0/13 again with zero visible errors on the sender. Any single-node reboot can break either direction; recovery requires resetting BOTH nodes within seconds of each other (fresh windows adopt each other's new epochs on first exchange - verified: paired gateway-bounce + T-Echo SMP reset restored 9/13 immediately).

FIX: persist the epoch (Zephyr settings/NVS) and increment it once per boot: epoch = (persisted+1) & 0xFF. Monotonic across reboots; the existing half-space (RFC 1982) comparison handles the 8-bit wrap (255->0 reads as newer), so it works indefinitely. This is exactly what the link_ctx.c comment already prescribes ('Callers with persisted epoch should call lichen_link_set_epoch() after init') - no receiver-side changes needed, cryptographically sound (no cross-epoch replay acceptance). Flash wear is one small write per boot. Note the receiver-side alternative (reinit window on epoch mismatch) is NOT sound: it would accept replays of captured old-epoch frames.

Until fixed, bench procedure: after rebooting/reflashing either the gateway or a puck, do a paired reset (bounce gateway CP2102 port, then SMP-reset the puck within ~2s), or expect a 50% chance of silent one-way deafness.
close_reason: HARDWARE VALIDATED 2026-07-17. Enabled CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK_EPOCH_PERSIST (+SETTINGS/NVS on each board's storage_partition) in the T-Echo puck and Heltec gateway confs; both build + flash clean. Authoritative gateway-side test (scratchpad/epoch_gw_view.py): opened the gateway console once (its single CP2102 reset gives a fresh replay window), then SMP-rebooted ONLY the T-Echo 8 times while watching the gateway for 'replay detected' on the T-Echo's post-reboot frames. Result: 0 replay rejections across all 8 reboots (gateway rebooted exactly once, as expected). Without persistence ~50% of reboots reject (expected ~4/8). The T-Echo's epoch now advances +1 per boot (persisted to NVS), staying ahead of the gateway's remembered counter, so a rebooted node is never seen as a replay. Cross-checked from the T-Echo side too: 14/16 reboots recovered the round trip with replay=0 on every cycle; the 2 non-recoveries were silent airtime losses (gateway accepted the GET, response lost to SF10 contention), not coin-flips. Both boards run persistence, covering both directions. Native_sim link_epoch test (4/4) proves the increment/wrap/persist logic; this proves the on-air outcome.
